methods.description: Analysis of bee diets using taxonomic and trait-based metrics to understand relationships with urban intensity, habitat amount, and resource availability.

steps.description: We performed taxonomic and trait-based metrics on the bee diets at the city and site levels.
steps.searchable_summary: perform taxonomic and trait-based metrics analysis on bee diets

steps.description: We computed the proportion of different plant taxa at the family, genus and species levels.
steps.searchable_summary: compute proportion of plant taxa at various taxonomic levels

steps.description: We calculated the species, genus and family richness and the Shannon diversity index.
steps.searchable_summary: calculate species, genus, family richness and Shannon diversity index

steps.description: We calculated the proportion of the different categories of the three studied traits.
steps.searchable_summary: calculate proportion of different trait categories

steps.description: For each of the four studied bees, we performed Pearson correlations to investigate the relationships between the taxonomic and the trait-based diet metrics with the proxies of urban intensity, habitat amount and resource availability.
steps.searchable_summary: perform Pearson correlations between diet metrics and urban proxies

steps.description: We calculated the pairwise correlations between cities for each bee species to study diet consistency.
steps.searchable_summary: calculate pairwise correlations between cities for diet consistency

steps.description: We assembled binary trophic interaction matrices between the four bee species and the plant taxa at the family, genus and species levels.

methods.description: Selection of study sites across five European cities based on urban green areas and connectivity metrics.

steps.description: We selected sites from the urban green areas mapped and defined in the pan-European Urban Atlas.
steps.searchable_summary: select sites from urban green areas using Urban Atlas

steps.description: We calculated connectivity using the proximity index (PI), which considers the area and the distance to all nearby patches with a favourable habitat.
We set the search radius to 5 km from each focal patch, the maximum value possible with the available cartography.
steps.searchable_summary: set search radius for connectivity calculation

steps.description: We maintained a minimum distance of 500 m between selected sites.
steps.searchable_summary: ensure minimum distance between selected sites

methods.description: Installation and management of trap-nests for sampling bees across study sites.

steps.description: At each site, we installed trap-nests in trees, and in three cases in other vertical structures.
We constructed trap-nests with reeds and cardboard tubes.
steps.searchable_summary: construct trap-nests using reeds and cardboard tubes

steps.description: We installed trap-nests at 2.5-3.5 m height with direct sunlight and SE or SW exposition.
steps.searchable_summary: install trap-nests at specified height and orientation

steps.description: We kept them in the field from January until October 2018.
steps.searchable_summary: maintain trap-nests in field for specified duration

steps.description: In October, we collected the trap-nests and stored them at c. 5°C until February 2019.
steps.searchable_summary: collect and store trap-nests at specified temperature

steps.description: We transferred them to a new room at ambient temperature to recreate spring-like conditions.
steps.searchable_summary: transfer trap-nests to ambient temperature for hatching

steps.description: Bees hatched and were identified to the species level from February to June 2019.
steps.searchable_summary: identify hatched bees to species level
